mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods yield negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of cases during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accommodative monetary policy combined with stable unemployment creates an optimal liquidity environment for equity valuation expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between low FEDFUNDS and stable UNRATE shows a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth below the 1.5% annualized threshold signals weakening economic momentum and impending earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust employment expansion and rising household income, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS against 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with stagnant industrial production creates a stagflationary environment that forces monetary tightening and compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).
